School.title: School
School.description: Represents a school using the SmartSApp onboarding platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the School entity.

fullName.description: The full name of the school.

pageName.description: The dynamically generated page name for the school (e.g., 'pe-ghana-international-school').

MeetingPage.title: MeetingPage
MeetingPage.description: Represents a dynamically generated meeting page for a specific school.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MeetingPage entity.

meetingTime.description: The date and time of the meeting.

heroImage.description: URL of the hero image for the meeting page.

heroText.description: Main text displayed in the hero section.

heroDescription.description: Descriptive text accompanying the hero text.

DownloadLink.title: DownloadLink
DownloadLink.description: Represents a download link for the SmartSApp mobile app on a specific platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DownloadLink entity.

platform.description: The platform for which the download link is provided (e.g., Android, iOS, Huawei).

url.description: The URL for downloading the app on the specified platform.

HelpVideo.title: HelpVideo
HelpVideo.description: Represents a helpful video resource for users.

id.description: Unique identifier for the HelpVideo entity.

title.description: The title of the help video.

url.description: The URL of the help video (e.g., YouTube link).

Testimonial.title: Testimonial
Testimonial.description: Represents a video testimonial from a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Testimonial entity.

name.description: The name of the person giving the testimonial.

title.description: Title or role of the person giving the testimonial.

videoUrl.description: The URL of the testimonial video.

definition.description: Stores school data. Each document represents a school using the SmartSApp onboarding platform.
Stores dynamically generated meeting pages for each school. Each document represents a specific meeting page associated with a school.

params.name: meetingPageId
params.description: Unique identifier for the meeting page.

definition.description: Stores download links for the SmartSApp mobile app for specific meeting pages.

params.name: meetingPageId
params.description: Unique identifier for the meeting page.

params.name: downloadLinkId
params.description: Unique identifier for the download link.

definition.description: Stores helpful video resources for users associated with a specific meeting page.

params.name: meetingPageId
params.description: Unique identifier for the meeting page.

params.name: helpVideoId
params.description: Unique identifier for the help video.

definition.description: Stores video testimonials from users associated with a specific meeting page.

params.name: meetingPageId
params.description: Unique identifier for the meeting page.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to support the SmartOnboard app's features, focusing on schools and dynamically generated meeting pages. The structure is optimized for simplicity and security. Schools are stored in a top-level `/schools` collection. Meeting pages are stored as subcollections under each school (`/schools/{schoolId}/meetingPages`). This hierarchical structure enforces ownership (a meeting page belongs to a school) and allows for efficient querying and data management.

This design ensures Authorization Independence by avoiding `get()` calls in security rules. Instead of relying on parent document data, necessary attributes for authorization (e.g., school administrators) would be denormalized into the `meetingPages` documents (if needed, based on future authorization requirements). Currently, since access control isn't explicitly specified for meeting pages, denormalization isn't added at this point but would be added if necessary in the future.

This structure supports QAPs (Rules are not Filters) by structuring the data in a way that allows straightforward security rules based on the path and, if required in the future, denormalized data. Listing meeting pages is secure because the rules can be applied at the collection level, ensuring that only authorized users can access specific meeting pages based on the `schoolId`.
